content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN integers.

You can perform the following operation as many times as you want (possibly 000):


Choose index iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N), and multiply AiA_iAi‚Äč by 222.
Find the **minimum** possible value of max‚Ā°(A)‚ąímin‚Ā°(A)\max(A) - \min(A)max(A)‚ąímin(A) you can achieve through the use of the operations.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains NNN - the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum possible value of max‚Ā°(A)‚ąímin‚Ā°(A)\max(A) - \min(A)max(A)‚ąímin(A) possible with the use of several operations.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : Choose i=3i = 3i=3, and multiply by 222. We get [2,2,3][2, 2, 3][2,2,3] which has max‚Ā°(A)‚ąímin‚Ā°(A)=1\max(A) - \min(A) = 1max(A)‚ąímin(A)=1.
Test Case 2 : Choose i=1i = 1i=1, and multiply by 222. We get [2,2,2][2, 2, 2][2,2,2] which has max‚Ā°(A)‚ąímin‚Ā°(A)=0\max(A) - \min(A) = 0max(A)‚ąímin(A)=0.
